commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.7.10: the frequency table — a chart of the printer's case redraws itself

Recast the basic 3-bar chart (invented values, rainbow gradients, no frame) into
THE FREQUENCY TABLE: English letter frequency — E T A O I N · S H R D L U, the
order of the printer's type case — redrawing one sort at a time. The empty ruled
table (value axis, dashed gridlines, per-cent ticks) is read first through the
read-beat; then each sort grows as a flat matte-jade column topped by its glyph
set in the specimen serif, the currently-set sort lifting to bright jade.

- ONE scalar (scaled = clamp(n.w/0.9)*N) drives bar height, the live SET count,
  the highlighted sort AND the caption [LAW:one-source-of-truth] — table, number
  and words can never disagree.
- One SVG (viewBox 200x100) so every bar provably stands on the shared axis;
  geometry derives from one baseline + value scale, no hand-placed y's.
- Wears the shared jade specimen vocabulary (8-strip crop-mark frame at positive
  inset, tracked-mono catalogue label); accent jade, tempo movement (12 sorts
  earn the longest breath).
- Distinct mechanic: a QUANTITATIVE graphic whose bars GROW — not annotate's
  static callouts, waffle's unit grid, count's ticking figures, draw's stroke.
- Pure scroll-driven; the live-bar fill transition is auto-gated by the global
  prefers-reduced-motion kill, no extra REDUCE_MOTION branch.

Verified in Chrome at 810px across the full scroll range: zero console errors.

mit-styling-aj1.7.9: the anatomy plate — annotation callouts land on a held specimen word (#32)

Recast the rejected POC annotate scene (a vague gradient terrain with
chat-bubble pins narrating 'Labels arrive as you scroll…') into THE ANATOMY
PLATE: the type designer's test word 'handgloves' held still in serif on its
four type-metric rules (ascender / x-height / baseline / descender), then a
draughtsman's callout LANDS for each glyph part — a target ring on the feature,
a thin leader to a tracked-mono term in the margin.

- Its OWN mechanic: static labels pinning onto ONE held still — no travelling
  boundary (wipe), no radial knockout (mask), and deliberately NO self-drawing
  stroke (draw): each callout settles in via opacity+lift. The satellite-
  annotation form turned into the specimen book's anatomy plate.
- Honest content replaces the meta narration: real anatomy terms, true one-line
  definitions, and an identity caption ('handgloves — set in Georgia') at rest.
- ONE scalar (shown) drives which callouts are on AND the live N/total readout
  [LAW:one-source-of-truth]; the metric-rule y's derive from one font-size +
  baseline, never hand-placed.
- Wears the shared specimen crop-mark frame (positive inset, the full-bleed
  convention); whole plate is one SVG so a leader provably touches its target.
- Serif face set in JS (the quoted-family inline trap); callouts land on n.w
  (dwell beat) so the word reads whole first. Pure scroll-driven, no new WAAPI.

Verified in Chrome at 810px across the node's range (~69346–72080px): 0/7
dwell, mid, 7/7 — every ring lands on its feature, zero console messages.
